author: Rama Nand, Dr.
title: Mi'yar-ul-khayalat
gloss: A booklet in two parts—the first part containing essays on subjects relating to moral and spiritual life of man and the second on matters relating to his bodily welfare.

title: Salana Report
gloss: Annual report of the Sadar Anjuman-i-Ahmadiya, Qádián, for 1908-09.
publisher: M. Muhammad Ali, Secretary of the Anjuman
notes: First Qadian imprint in our 1910 extractions — the Ahmadiyya movement's own Magazine Press reporting its institutional year (secretary M. Muhammad Ali, the future Lahori-branch leader); bureaucratic self-documentation entering the colonial registry.

author: Sant Ram, Lala
title: Josh-i-Ghairat
gloss: An appeal addressed to the Hindus in general and to the Bhatias in particular, in which the writer suggests reformation of the bad custom of marrying young girls to old men for the sake of money.
notes: Anti-bride-price reform appeal targeted at the Bhatia caste specifically — caste-addressed social reform print.

author: Shankar Bihari, Lala
title: Jadid Tariq-i-Zaraat
gloss: A small tract briefly describing the mode of cultivation according to modern scientific methods.
notes: Scientific-agriculture improvement tract from Lyallpur, the flagship canal colony.

author: Sundar Singh, Professor
title: Guldasta-i-Harmonium, Hissa-i-Awwal
gloss: A booklet containing instructions for playing on the harmonium, Part I.
copyright: Sundar Singh, Amritsar; No. 185, dated the 3rd December, 1910.
notes: 'Bouquet of the Harmonium', Part I — 5,000 copies of harmonium self-instruction in Urdu, copyrighted by a 'Professor' author: the imported reed instrument being institutionalized in Punjabi musical pedagogy (key evidence for the music-colonization thread; pairs with Rag Binod, p18 s2). Title-page date January 1910 but registered this quarter.

author: Altaf Husain Hali, Khawaja, Shams-ul-Ulama
title: Hubb-i-Watan
notes: Hali's 'Love of Country' in the Vakil Trading Co. reprint series — the canon-formation operation seen in Q3 continues into Q4; Urdu—Poetry serials resume at 59.

author: Altaf Husain Hali, Khawaja, Shams-ul-Ulama
title: Munazara-i-Rahm-o-Insaf
gloss: A dialogue between Mercy and Justice, in verse.
notes: Author dash-continuation of s59; second Vakil Trading Hali title registered the same day.
